because.text: Fourth Lean front and Qwen's explicit ask (Book I determinism/totality). EvalMachine.lean models the v0.5 hash-thunk machine faithfully - leftmost-outermost, lazy left-spine, size-priced ATP, redex recognition by hash - and is built ON MachineBytes so recognition rides the PROVEN byte/hash layer, not a re-axiomatized one. Totality is definitional (step well-founded on sizeOf, eval fuel-indexed structural recursion: evalHash is a total function, no partial/unsafe); determinism is definitional (a function). step_bounds (fun_induction + grind) yields step_cost_le (cost<=remaining) and step_cost_pos (cost>=1, the §3.4 minimum - no zero-cost stall); eval_spent_le proves spent<=atp for ALL terms and budgets. Bridge: no-sorry guard + compile + the executed Lean evaluator (EvalRun.lean) vs the oracle on all 33 eval vectors, result NodeHash AND atp_spent byte-exact, incl. Omega divergence (500 ATP -> exhausted), R-S size-pricing, genesis-intrinsic, store isolation, stuck forms. Maintainer wrote the model, bridge, and eval_spent_le; delegated only the step_bounds case-bash (a subagent hit its session limit and reverted, maintainer closed it directly with fun_induction+grind); cold-ran the full bridge + all three prior-front regressions on a fresh master checkout on the mini before filing. Remaining: the step-tag classifier for row-by-row SizeBound correspondence; Rust production impl.

subject.note: Executable law: the Book I evaluator is mechanized (Lean 4) - determinism/totality definitional, spent<=atp a theorem, 33-vector oracle differential
